---
title: "Dixie aster (Sericocarpus tortifolius)"
slug: "sericocarpus-tortifolius"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/354685724/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Dixie aster"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Asterales"
tax_family: "Asteraceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Asterales", "Asteraceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/354685724/medium.jpg" alt="Dixie aster" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/billpranty">
billpranty</a> (CC BY) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/200974523">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Asterales
-   **Family:** Asteraceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=131178)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not available
-   **Rarity note:** Common across Florida.

## Natural history notes

A perennial wildflower characterized by its narrow, often twisted leaves and clusters of small white flower heads. In Florida, it is found in dry to mesic habitats such as sandhills, scrub, and open woodlands. It typically blooms in fall and provides a nectar source for a variety of pollinators.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/131178-Sericocarpus-tortifolius)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/3207)
